project.name: daily-llm-viewer
project.goal: Streamlit-based analysis tool for the LLM daily test results. Supersedes _old_viewer.py + _old_ingest.py + _old_schema.sql.
project.language_preference: ko-KR for conversation; English for code/comments/docs

pipeline.viewer_consumes: DuckDB read-only via queries.py; Streamlit app in app.py

daily/viewer/schema.sql.purpose: DuckDB schema. Source of truth. ensure_schema() runs this on every ingest.
notes: perf stores RAW in_token/out_token. No bucketing at write time (per user requirement B).

perf_stats uses correlated subqueries for rolling median + MAD over a 30-day back-window. Kept for legacy point-vs-band helpers; Regression tab now recomputes the selected-series rolling band from purpose-filtered perf_flat rows so personal PR results do not pollute the chart.
runs.rawlog_path stores a FILE PATH, not the log body (old schema stored the whole log in a TEXT column; removed to keep DB small).
runs.source_format is 'old' | 'new' — keep this for debugging data provenance.

daily/viewer/profiles/default.yaml.purpose: Replacement for FIXED_ROW_ORDER. Controls row order in Excel-paste tab.

match_spec_semantics.*: any value (token-agnostic; used for SD/LCM pipelines)

daily/viewer/profiles/default.yaml.extension: Add e.g. profiles/iGPU.yaml for machine-specific rows. Load via `python -m viewer.ingest.cli --profile <yaml>`. Sidebar picks between loaded profiles.

daily/viewer/ingest/record.py.purpose: RunRecord + DeviceRecord + PerfRow dataclasses. Format-neutral — loaders fill these, writer consumes them.
daily/viewer/ingest/record.py.do_not: Add format-specific fields here. Put them on the loader.

exports: file_hash (sha256 content hash of source file)

daily/viewer/ingest/loader_new.py.purpose: summary.json -> RunRecord. Raw token counts preserved.
dropped_handlers: whisper_base (TestWhisperBase) — removed in commit 708a853; no longer part of the daily signal
daily/viewer/ingest/loader_new.py.meta_handling: summary.meta is optional — falls back to filename stamp + parent-dir machine + generated_at ts. Early summary files (before meta landed) still work.

daily/viewer/ingest/loader_old.py.pickle_trick: _TolerantUnpickler.find_class replaces missing classes (test_cases.TestBenchmark etc.) with _UnknownClass stubs. Key tuple is (model, precision, TestClass) — we read TestClass.__name__ to dispatch to the correct extractor.

class_handlers.TestStableDiffusion: _sd_perf_ms (legacy C++ binary — ms in pickle, divided by 1000 at ingest)

daily/viewer/ingest/loader_old.py.version_parsing: OV version pulled from filename 'daily.<stamp>.<ov_version>.pickle' AND from .report text (Purpose + OpenVINO line). Report text is preferred when present.
daily/viewer/ingest/loader_old.py.sd_unit_normalization: All SD pipelines land in the DB as unit='s' regardless of source class. Legacy ms pickles are divided by 1000 at load time. This removes the 1000x ambiguity that made SD-XL pipeline look like milliseconds in the viewer.

daily/viewer/ingest/writer.py.purpose: DuckDB upsert (transactional per RunRecord) + display profile loader.
daily/viewer/ingest/writer.py.dedup: perf is replaced wholesale per run (DELETE + INSERT). Duplicate PKs within a run are deduplicated to last-wins.

daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.purpose: Single CLI; --root scans, --input handles one file.
daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.auto_detect: _classify: *.summary.json -> new; *.pickle (with sibling .report) -> old; *.report alone -> old if sibling pickle exists, else skip.
daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.progress: Inline progress bar. Failures collected and reported at end; not fatal.
daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.profile_load: Each run (re)loads profiles/default.yaml into display_rows unless --skip-profile.

daily/viewer/queries.py.purpose: DuckDB helpers used by app.py. All return pandas DataFrames.

funcs.list_runs: runs metadata for one machine, newest first
funcs.build_excel_matrix: Cross-join display_rows x selected runs, LEFT JOIN perf_with_buckets, then manual pivot (pandas pivot_table drops NaN index and explodes on dropna=False)
funcs.extra_rows: Perf rows in selected runs NOT covered by the display profile — QA aid
funcs.series_history: Time series of one (machine, model, precision, in_token, out_token, exec_mode), with rolling median/MAD recomputed from purpose-filtered perf_flat rows. Used for the trend plot in the Regression tab.
funcs.trend_regressions: NEW — per-series comparison: median of recent window vs median of baseline window. worsening_pct is sign-normalised (positive = worse regardless of ms vs FPS). This is what the Regression tab drives off.
funcs.regressions_for_run: OLD point-vs-band flags. Kept for possible mail-alert reuse but not wired into the UI any more.

tabs.Excel: Select runs -> wide matrix (profile rows x run stamps) + tab-separated paste block + 'extra rows' expander
tabs.Regression: MERGED tab (was separate Trend + Regressions). Shows a ranked table of series by worsening%, plus a one-series-at-a-time trend plot for the selected row. Baseline median + recent median rendered as Scatter traces with legend entries; rolling ±2σ band drawn with a visible fill (rgba 0.28) so it reads on bright monitors.
tabs.Geomean: exec_mode x in_bucket x out_bucket geomean trend + band + latest-point alert (user-requested)
tabs.Noise: Per-series CV table sorted desc (iGPU diagnostics)

sidebar.machine_filter: 'Daily machines only' checkbox (default True). When True, dropdown is filtered by DAILY_MACHINES constant (dg2alderlake, MTL-01, ARLH-01, BMG-02, LNL-03, LNL-04, DUT4580PTLH, DUT6047BMGFRD). Falls back to full list if none intersect.
sidebar.default_filter: Excel tab purpose filter defaults to 'daily_CB timer' via DEFAULT_RUN_FILTER constant.
sidebar.days_slider: 7-60 day range for trend history. All tab-local days inputs (Geomean, Noise) capped at 60 for consistency.

daily/viewer/app.py.unit_display: Every user-visible numeric carries its unit: table median columns render as '8.060 s', trend heading shows '[s]', caption prints 'Recent median = 8.060 s'. Prevents SD pipeline seconds from being mis-read as ms.
daily/viewer/app.py.caching: All queries wrapped with @st.cache_data keyed on DB mtime to auto-invalidate on re-ingest.
daily/viewer/app.py.config_source: DAILY_DB env var or `-- --db <path>` trailing arg (streamlit swallows its own flags).

daily/run.py.purpose: Daily suite entry — runs pytest, builds reports, ships mail/xlsx.
daily/run.py.exit_policy: Always returns 0 if the run completed end-to-end (report built). Test failures are reflected in JSON summary / mail, not exit code. Only infra failures (no pytest JSON produced) propagate a non-zero rc. Changed per user request; relied on by Jenkins to distinguish 'test failed' from 'run itself broke'.

files.daily/viewer/perf_rows.py: Used by xlsx_update.py path (flatten summary -> lookup dict). Kept as-is. NOTE: this one DOES bucket ('short'/'long') for the xlsx template — do not reuse for DB ingest (which must keep raw tokens).
files.daily/viewer/xlsx_update.py: Separate xlsx append path invoked from run.py. Independent of the DuckDB pipeline.

rejected: Python list in code (no version control per-row, harder to diff)
design_decisions.why: User adds/removes models frequently. YAML is reviewable in git; DB copy is for fast JOINs in queries. Match spec ('short'/'long'/'*'/<int>') is expressive enough without code changes.

design_decisions.topic: Raw tokens in DB vs bucketed
design_decisions.user_request: B — ingest is data-as-is; viewer does the bucketing
design_decisions.implementation: perf_with_buckets view derives in_bucket/out_bucket. Threshold lives in SQL — change the view to retune, no re-ingest needed.

design_decisions.choice: Two-window median comparison: recent window median vs baseline window median
rejected: Single-point robust z-score — user feedback: they want to see whether the recent BLOCK is drifting, not whether today is an outlier. Single-point tests flip on any blip and are useless when data is noisy or occasionally corrupted.
mean/std z-score — too sensitive to iGPU outliers
design_decisions.why: iGPU runs are noisy and individual data points are sometimes contaminated. Comparing medians of two time windows (default: last 7d vs 21d prior) washes both out while still catching slow drift. This matches common benchmark tools (ASV / pytest-benchmark) which emphasize median-like robust summaries and percentage thresholds rather than reacting to a single point.
design_decisions.direction_normalisation: worsening_pct is signed so positive always means 'worse' — ms/s/%: +pct when recent>baseline; FPS/tps: +pct when recent<baseline. worsening_z uses baseline MAD (sigma ~= 1.4826*MAD) so recent noise does not hide or inflate the comparison. The UI sorts by threshold-normalised severity = max(worsening_pct/pct_threshold, worsening_z/z_threshold).
design_decisions.purpose_filter: Regression summary and selected-series history are filtered to purpose containing 'daily_CB timer' so personal PR runs do not pollute baseline/recent windows. The chart rolling band is recomputed from filtered perf_flat rows, not from perf_stats, because perf_stats is an all-purpose view.
design_decisions.ui: Single merged 'Regression' tab. Ranked table (worst first by threshold-normalised severity) + one trend chart per selected row. See app.py:_tab_regression.
design_decisions.supersedes: The earlier rolling z-score approach (queries.regressions_for_run) is still defined but unused by the UI.

design_decisions.choice: Enforce single-series plot in the Regression tab
design_decisions.why: User feedback: mixing models with wildly different value ranges on one chart makes it unreadable. Table-plus-single-chart is the pattern that scales.
design_decisions.implementation: Row selection in the summary table drives which series is plotted. Default = row 0 (worst drift). The y-axis range has a minimum relative span so tiny stable differences (e.g. 31.6 ms vs 31.95 ms) are not over-zoomed into visually noisy fluctuations.

design_decisions.problem_observed: Legacy TestStableDiffusion pickle stored ms; newer TestStableDiffusionGenai/DGfxE2eAi pickles stored seconds. The original _sd_perf lumped them all into unit='ms', so SD-XL pipeline (actually 8 s) showed up as 8 ms in comparisons.
design_decisions.fix: Split into _sd_perf_ms (legacy, /1000) and _sd_perf_sec (new, as-is). All SD pipelines now land as unit='s'.
design_decisions.remediation: Re-ingested with --force after the fix. Confirmed: 0 remaining ms-labeled SD rows in DB.

design_decisions.topic: Units visible in every UI surface
design_decisions.choice: Attach unit suffix to table cells, plot headings, and captions
design_decisions.why: Raw numbers without units caused the SD-XL confusion above. User should never have to remember which test type is in which unit.
design_decisions.implementation: Regression table builds display-only 'recent'/'baseline' columns like '8.060 s'; raw numeric columns hidden via column_config but retained for plot/caption code paths.

design_decisions.action: Removed handlers from loader_new.py + loader_old.py, deleted existing rows from DB.
design_decisions.note: whisper-large-v3 (NOT Whisper base) is a different model in the GenAI pipeline and is kept.

design_decisions.user_request: C — user explicitly wants geomean-level trend alerts (not just per-series)
design_decisions.implementation: geomean_trend() groups by (machine, exec_mode, in_bucket, out_bucket). Tab computes median + MAD of the geomean series itself and shows a banner if latest point falls outside ±z·σ AND ±pct%.

design_decisions.choice: Hardcoded DAILY_MACHINES tuple in app.py with sidebar checkbox (default True)
rejected: DB-backed list (operational set doesn't need runtime mutation and git is the right source of truth)
design_decisions.fallback: If the checkbox filter leaves the dropdown empty (fresh DB), falls back to the full machine list.

design_decisions.user_request: Always return 0 when the run completes, not when tests pass. Infra failure vs test failure must be distinguishable.
design_decisions.choice: Return 0 after build_reports succeeds, regardless of pytest rc. Still propagates non-zero if pytest never produced the JSON (real infra break).
design_decisions.rationale: Jenkins treats non-zero as 'build broke'. The team wants 'test failed' signalled via mail/report, not Jenkins.

design_decisions.why: Including iso_ts makes re-runs minutes apart distinct even if the file stem somehow collides.

design_decisions.choice: sha256 content hash of the source file (pickle or summary.json)
design_decisions.why: Stable across path changes / rsyncs / backups. Replaces the old (path|size|mtime) scheme which broke on copy.
design_decisions.cost: Hashing is I/O-bound but amortised: 6006 files in 57s on /var/www/html/daily.

design_decisions.choice: Store PATH only (rawlog_path); never the body
design_decisions.old_behavior: _old_schema.sql stored the full text in runs.rawlog — this exploded the DB
design_decisions.mitigation: If log access is needed from the UI, open the file from the path. Viewer doesn't do this yet.

design_decisions.why: Legacy pickles reference test_cases.test_benchmark.TestBenchmark etc. We don't want to import that package into the viewer. We only need the class NAME to dispatch extractors.

design_decisions.choice: One transaction per RunRecord (runs + system_devices + perf together)
design_decisions.why: Partial-state bugs in the old ingest (separate inserts) were painful. Run is the natural unit.
Streamlit cache invalidates when any cache key changes. Passing mtime means re-ingest auto-refreshes all cached queries without manual invalidation.

design_decisions.choice: xaxis autorange reversed — newest on the left
design_decisions.why: User request: matches the team's convention of reading newest-first.

logic: TRY_CAST(spec AS INTEGER) = token -> exact numeric match
match_rules_in_sql.note: All three are OR'd. TRY_CAST returns NULL for non-integer strings, which cleanly fails the comparison.

how_to_run.launch_viewer: cd daily && streamlit run viewer/app.py -- --db viewer/bench.duckdb
how_to_run.python_env: Use /home/sungeunk/miniforge3/envs/daily/bin/python (conda 'daily' env). System python3 doesn't have duckdb/streamlit.

2026_04_25_initial.new_format_ingest: 1 file (daily.20260422_0903.summary.json from dg2raptorlake) -> 4 perf rows. OK.
2026_04_25_initial.old_format_ingest: 1 file (daily.20250224_0104 from res/) -> 74 perf rows. OK.
2026_04_25_initial.bulk_old_ingest: 453 files from /var/www/html/daily/LNL-02 -> 452 runs, 27k perf rows, 4.7s, 0 failures.
2026_04_25_initial.viewer_smoke: All 5 tabs called headlessly against real data — no uncaught exceptions.

2026_04_25_sd_unit_fix.unit_audit: Final check: 0 ms-labeled SD rows remain; all SD/whisper/lcm/flux pipelines carry unit='s' with sensible magnitudes.
2026_04_25_sd_unit_fix.regression_tab_trace: 4 plot traces confirmed: value / baseline median / recent median / ±2σ band — all as Scatter with distinct legend entries.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: Viewer doesn't open raw logs from rawlog_path yet.
known_gaps_and_future_work.why_not_done: Not requested. One-liner to add in Regression tab or a new 'Raw Log' tab.
All modules were smoke-tested against real data. If the codebase grows, pytest on _common.py (stamp/hash parsers), loader_new/loader_old (fixture files), and queries (synthetic DB) would be worth it.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: perf_stats still uses correlated subqueries (O(n*m) conceptually).
known_gaps_and_future_work.cost_today: ~0.1s at current scale (27k rows, then 6006 runs on full tree).
known_gaps_and_future_work.when_to_revisit: If query time exceeds 1s, rewrite as a Python-side precomputation that writes into a cached `perf_stats_cached` table on ingest.

known_gaps_and_future_work.why_not_done: Out of scope for this change. Hook would be: after run.py's send_mail, run queries.trend_regressions for the latest machine and append a section to the mail body.

known_gaps_and_future_work.why_kept: Might still be useful for mail alerts or a per-run QA view. If it hasn't been touched by 2026-Q3, delete it.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: Profile CLI currently always loads default.yaml on every ingest unless --skip-profile.
known_gaps_and_future_work.possible_refactor: Make --profile additive by default: skip-if-exists semantics, or always upsert only the named profile without touching others.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: machine name for pickles ingested from non-canonical locations
known_gaps_and_future_work.why: loader_old.py derives machine from parent dir name. If a pickle sits in a dir not named after the machine, the value is wrong (seen with res/ fixture -> machine='res').
known_gaps_and_future_work.mitigation: Acceptable for real /var/www/html/daily/<MACHINE>/ layout. If we need to rescue fixtures, add a --machine override to cli.py.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: Display profile dropdown shows only 'default' today
known_gaps_and_future_work.why: Only one YAML exists. Consider hiding the dropdown until a second profile lands, or pre-ship an iGPU-focused profile to motivate the machinery.

units.s: seconds (SD / LCM / whisper / flux pipelines), lower_is_better
units.%: percent, treated as lower_is_better for regression direction

user_profile.role: OpenVINO GPU plugin developer; owns the daily benchmark pipeline.
preferences: Korean for conversation; English for code/doc artefacts.
Autonomous execution preferred once direction is agreed — do not pause for confirmation on each substep.
Appreciates explicit trade-off analysis when multiple approaches exist.
Reads newest-first: trend plots have the recent end on the left.
